entries.value: Target lead conversion rate is >20%. Calculate as converted_leads / total_leads * 100 over a rolling 90-day window. Segment by source — referral leads typically convert at 2-3x cold outreach. Below 10% suggests qualification criteria need tightening.

Target average sales cycle is <45 days from first contact to closed-won. Track by deal size tier — enterprise deals typically run 60-120 days. Cycles lengthening over time signals market headwinds or qualification issues.

Pipeline velocity = (number of deals * avg deal value * win rate) / avg sales cycle length. Target >$50K/week for SMB pipelines. Declining velocity with stable deal count means deals are getting smaller or slower.

Target win rate is >30% of qualified opportunities. Calculate as closed_won / (closed_won + closed_lost) * 100. Exclude deals still open. Track by stage entry — proposals that reach negotiation should close at >50%.

Track average deal size trend over rolling quarters. Growing deal size with stable win rate indicates successful up-market movement. Declining deal size may mean pricing pressure or shift to smaller accounts.

Customer acquisition cost (CAC) = total sales & marketing spend / new customers acquired. Healthy CAC payback period is <12 months. CAC should be <33% of customer lifetime value (LTV). Track CAC by channel to optimize spend.

Flag deals with no activity in 14+ days as stale. Deals in discovery/qualification stages stale after 10 days. Deals in negotiation stage stale after 21 days. Stale deals have 3x lower close probability.

Follow up with new leads within 5 minutes for 9x higher conversion. After initial contact: follow up at 1 day, 3 days, 7 days, then weekly. After 5 no-responses, move to nurture sequence. Never ghost — always close the loop.

Discovery: initial conversations to understand needs. Qualification: confirming budget, authority, need, timeline (BANT). Proposal: formal offer sent. Negotiation: terms being discussed. Closed Won/Lost: terminal states.

Use BANT framework: Budget (can they afford it?), Authority (are you talking to the decision maker?), Need (is there a real problem?), Timeline (is there urgency?). Require at least 3 of 4 BANT criteria before moving to proposal stage.
